notes: Rocha's first solo credit · 2004 · a 2-hour meditation piece.  One beach, one tide cycle, one walk.

shelf.shelf_condition: pristine except the spine, which is sun-faded to almost white · it lived on a windowsill
shelf.provenance_note: A mutual friend told Olaf that Ines Rocha specifically wanted him to have a copy.  He never found out why.  He played it once a year, in October.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: Rocha made it alone over six months of evenings after Estuary 3 shipped.  One beach rendered at four times of tide.  The only text in the game is the last line.  She has never said what the beach is, but the drift-line debris includes a specific child's shoe that also appears in Fey Faire's questionnaire art.

acts_deferred_note: Playable content authoring deferred.  Design intent: a 2-hour real-time walk, tide as the only clock, one ending.
